code-of-conduct ﻿ Skip to main content Company Policy Code of Conduct Our commitment to integrity, respect, and ethical business practices — guiding every interaction with colleagues, clients, vendors, and the communities we serve. This Code of Conduct reflects the values Forsys was built on — integrity, respect, and a genuine commitment to doing right by our people, our clients, and our partners. It guides how we operate every day, in every interaction. — Jaya Prasad Vejendla, Forsys Leadership · July 2021 Section I Introduction This Forsys Code of Conduct guides all employees in upholding ethical standards across every interaction — with colleagues, clients, vendors, and regulatory agencies. It sets the foundation for how we conduct ourselves as a company and as individuals representing Forsys. Section II Policy All employees are required to conduct business honestly, avoid conflicts of interest, and comply with all applicable laws and regulations. Forsys expects the highest standards of professional conduct from everyone who represents the company. Section III Report Violations Employees who suspect a violation of this Code must report it to their supervisor, manager, or HR. All good-faith reports are protected — retaliation against anyone who reports a concern is strictly prohibited. Section IV Ethical Business Practices Forsys conducts all business with the highest ethical standards. Our core values define how we work: Committed to Customers Deliver Results Passion for Innovation Uncompromised Quality & Transparency Corporate Governance Respect Bribery, inappropriate gifts, and entertainment that could create unfair advantages are strictly prohibited. Section V Conflict of Interest Employees must avoid situations where personal interests conflict with the interests of Forsys. Restrictions apply to outside employment, personal relationships with coworkers, and gift acceptance. The maximum value for gifts is $50 / ₹1,000. Section VI Environmental & Health Safety All employees must adhere to environmental laws and safety regulations. Safety violations must be reported immediately. Forsys is committed to maintaining a safe and healthy work environment for all. Section VII Privacy Forsys is committed to protecting personal data and maintaining the integrity of private information — for employees, clients, and partners alike. All employees must handle personal data with care and in accordance with applicable privacy laws. Section VIII Email & Internet Use Company systems are business property. Internet and email usage may be monitored. Transmission of confidential information without appropriate security measures is prohibited. Employees are expected to use company resources responsibly and professionally. Section IX Discrimination and Harassment Forsys maintains a zero-tolerance policy for discrimination and harassment in any form. Prohibited conduct includes: Unwanted sexual advances or requests for sexual favors Unwanted touching or physical contact Derogatory comments based on race, gender, religion, age, disability, or any protected characteristic Creating or contributing to a hostile work environment Retaliation against anyone who reports harassment Section X Workplace Violence Forsys maintains a zero-tolerance policy for workplace violence. Threats, intimidation, or physical assaults of any kind — against employees, clients, or visitors — are strictly prohibited and will result in immediate disciplinary action. Section XI Weapons Prohibition Firearms and weapons are prohibited on all Forsys facilities without explicit authorization. This applies to all employees, contractors, and visitors. Section XII Illegal Drugs and Alcohol The distribution, possession, or use of illegal substances is prohibited on company premises or during any business activity. Employees must not report to work or conduct business while under the influence of alcohol or illegal drugs. Section XIII Prohibited Substances Alcohol consumption is not permitted on company property. Smoking is restricted to designated areas only. Employees who appear impaired will be denied entry to the workplace. Section XIV Government Cooperation Employees must fully cooperate with

XII Illegal Drugs and Alcohol The distribution, possession, or use of illegal substances is prohibited on company premises or during any business activity. Employees must not report to work or conduct business while under the influence of alcohol or illegal drugs. Section XIII Prohibited Substances Alcohol consumption is not permitted on company property. Smoking is restricted to designated areas only. Employees who appear impaired will be denied entry to the workplace. Section XIV Government Cooperation Employees must fully cooperate with any lawful government investigations. Destruction of documents, false statements, and obstruction of any investigation are strictly prohibited and may result in immediate termination and legal action. Section XV Dress Code Forsys requires professional appearance and adherence to a formal dress code policy during business hours and client interactions. Casual attire is permitted on Fridays, subject to good judgment and professionalism. Section XVI Health and Safety Rules All employees are responsible for maintaining safe work environments. This includes: Reporting hazards, unsafe conditions, or near-miss incidents promptly Following all safety protocols and emergency procedures Participating in safety training as required Not engaging in activities that compromise the safety of others Section XVII Use of Company Resources Equipment, supplies, and company assets are the property of Forsys. Personal use, unauthorized transfer of assets, and use of company resources for political activities are prohibited. Employees are expected to use all resources responsibly and efficiently. Section XVIII Privacy, Confidentiality & Non-Disclosure All employees are required to sign Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDAs). Strict protection is required for: Trade secrets and proprietary business information Client data, contracts, and project details Personnel records and compensation information Any information designated as confidential by Forsys leadership Section XIX Immigration Law Compliance Police verification and required document filing must be completed for all employees on international assignments. Forsys complies fully with the immigration laws of all countries in which we operate. Section XX Disciplinary Procedures Violations of this Code will result in progressive disciplinary action, which may include verbal warnings, written warnings, suspension without pay, or termination of employment. Immediate dismissal may occur for severe violations including fraud, harassment, or violence. Section XXI Code Compliance All employees are expected to understand and comply with this Code of Conduct. Supervisors and managers bear additional responsibility for ensuring their teams understand and follow these guidelines. Section XXII Reporting Violations Employees must report any actual or perceived violations of this Code to HR. All reports will be treated with discretion and confidentiality. Non-retaliation protections apply to all good-faith reporters. Section XXIII Zero-Tolerance Policy Violations of this Code may result in immediate employment termination. Retaliation against whistleblowers is treated as a severe violation and will be prosecuted to the fullest extent permitted by law.

privacy-policy ﻿ Skip to main content Legal Privacy Policy How Forsys Inc. collects, uses, and protects your personal information when you use our website and services. This Privacy Policy covers forsysinc.com and related services. Forsys, Inc., a California corporation, is committed to protecting your privacy online. By accessing or using our site, you agree to be bound by all terms and conditions of this policy. Forsys reserves the right to modify this policy at any time, with changes taking effect upon notice via email or website posting. 1. What Information Do We Collect? We collect two types of information from visitors to our site: Personally Identifiable Information (PII) — collected when you register, order products or services, submit content, post in discussion forums, enter contests, complete surveys, or provide feedback. This may include: First and last name Mailing address and email address Employer, job title, and department Phone and fax numbers Credit card numbers (for transactions) Non-Personally Identifiable Information — collected automatically as you use the site, including the URL you came from, your browser type, IP address, and navigation patterns. 2. How Is Your Information Collected and Used? Information is collected at various points including service registration, special offer sign-ups, email or form submissions, and credit card transactions. Personally Identifiable Information is used to: Provide contracted services and fill orders Enhance site operations and improve marketing efforts Analyze site usage statistics and customize content Deliver administrative notices and resolve disputes Enforce our agreements with you Non-Personally Identifiable Information is used to diagnose server problems, administer the site, gather demographic data, and provide summary advertising reports. 3. Who Receives Your Information? We do not sell, trade, or rent your Personally Identifiable Information to others. We may share your information only with service partners under confidentiality agreements — including credit card processors, delivery services, and customer service providers. The use of your information by these partners is governed by their own privacy policies. Forsys complies with all law enforcement requests, court orders, and subpoenas. We reserve the right to report unlawful activities to law enforcement. Non-personally identifiable information may be shared with third-party advertisers regarding sales and traffic patterns, without identifying individuals. 4. How Can You Update or Correct Your Information? You can update your information through your online account or by contacting us at privacy@forsysinc.com (include your name, address, and email). Please note that information associated with past transactions cannot be deleted. Backup systems may retain residual copies of your information. 5. Your Choices Regarding Data Collection You can opt out of marketing email offers and direct mail at any time through your account settings. By modifying your browser preferences, you can choose to accept all cookies, be notified when a cookie is set, or reject all cookies. Please note that rejecting all cookies may impair certain site functionality. 6. How We Protect Your Information Your account is password-protected with an encrypted password, unique customer ID, and access limited to authorized personnel only. Your Personally Identifiable Information is encrypted during transmission to prevent unauthorized viewing. Credit card and fulfillment processing is handled by established banking and distribution institutions. However, no data transmission over the Internet or any wireless network can be guaranteed to be 100% secure. By using our site, you acknowledge the inherent security limitations of the Internet and that data may potentially be intercepted during transmission. 7. Cookies Cookies are stored on your device to facilitate a customized site experience. Cookie data may include a randomly assigned user ID, your country, and first name. Third-party advertisers may place separate cookies that collect non-personally identifiable data such as IP

data transmission over the Internet or any wireless network can be guaranteed to be 100% secure. By using our site, you acknowledge the inherent security limitations of the Internet and that data may potentially be intercepted during transmission. 7. Cookies Cookies are stored on your device to facilitate a customized site experience. Cookie data may include a randomly assigned user ID, your country, and first name. Third-party advertisers may place separate cookies that collect non-personally identifiable data such as IP address, browser type, and zip code. No personally identifiable information is provided to ad servers. 8. Third-Party Site Privacy Policies This Privacy Policy applies only to forsysinc.com. Other sites accessible through our site have their own privacy policies and data collection practices. Forsys is not responsible for the policies or practices of third parties. 9. Other Privacy Considerations Age Requirement: You must be at least 18 years old to use this site. We do not knowingly collect, use, or disclose Personally Identifiable Information about visitors under 18 years of age. Public Forums: Information shared in discussion forums or public areas of the site can be collected and used by third parties. Such activities are beyond Forsys's control. User Responsibility: You are solely responsible for maintaining the secrecy of your password and account information. Contact Us For privacy-related questions or concerns, please contact our Privacy Compliance Officer: Privacy Compliance Officer Email: privacycompliance@forsysinc.com General inquiries: info@forsysinc.com

sustainability-policy ﻿ Skip to main content Legal & ESG Environmental Sustainability Policy Our commitment to minimizing environmental impact and building a more sustainable future — for our company, our clients, and our planet. Forsys is a California-based solutions company founded in 2015, recognized for expertise in quote-to-cash consulting. We emphasize strategy, design, and technological engineering with a commitment to customized solutions and sustainable outcomes. We recognize the importance of environmental sustainability and commit to minimizing our environmental impact as a responsible corporate citizen. As a global software company, Forsys leverages our knowledge, expertise, and intellectual property to help customers, suppliers, and partners in their journey towards achieving their environmental sustainability goals. 1. Resource Efficiency We are committed to improving operational efficiency for key resources including energy and water across all our facilities and operations. Improve operational efficiency for energy, water, and other key resources Integrate environmental considerations into facility design and enhancements Monitor resource usage and set measurable improvement targets 2. Compliance and Continuous Improvement Forsys is committed to meeting and exceeding applicable environmental laws and regulations across every region in which we operate. Comply with all relevant environmental laws and regulations globally Set, monitor, and review environmental objectives and targets on an ongoing basis Continuously seek opportunities to improve our environmental performance 3. Energy Efficiency We are dedicated to reducing our energy consumption and carbon footprint through thoughtful practices and the adoption of energy-efficient technologies. Promote energy-saving technologies and practices throughout operations Reduce energy consumption and carbon footprint across all offices Encourage responsible employee energy use with training and awareness programs 4. Waste Management We adopt sustainable waste management practices that reduce generation at the source and maximize recycling and reuse opportunities. Reduce waste generation and promote recycling and reuse programs Ensure safe disposal of hazardous waste in compliance with regulations Educate employees on waste reduction and provide segregation facilities 5. Sustainable Procurement Environmental responsibility extends to our supply chain. We integrate sustainability considerations into every procurement decision. Integrate environmental considerations into procurement decisions Prefer suppliers and partners who share our commitment to sustainability Evaluate vendor environmental performance as part of our selection process 6. Environmental Awareness and Training We invest in educating our team so every Forsys employee understands their role in advancing our environmental commitments. Raise employee awareness about environmental issues and our company commitments Provide training and resources for contributing to environmental objectives Foster a culture where every team member is an active sustainability advocate Implementation & Accountability Responsibility for implementing this policy rests with our Global HR team. Regular reviews ensure effectiveness and continued relevance as our business evolves. All employees are encouraged to contribute ideas for environmental improvement. Global HR — Sustainability Lead Email: hr@forsysinc.com General inquiries: info@forsysinc.com JP Jaya Prasad Vejendla CEO, Forsys Inc. · Adopted January 1, 2024

ai-agents-development ﻿ Skip to main content Services — Technology AI & Agents Development Forsys is an early adopter and Agentforce implementation specialist — building autonomous AI agents that handle lead routing, case deflection, quote generation, contract review, and renewal management with no human intervention required. Build Your AI Agents → Explore Our Approach The Agentforce Build Framework From agent strategy to autonomous production agents Forsys has deployed AI agents across 200+ Salesforce and Oracle engagements — we know how to build agents that actually work in production. Forsys builds AI agents on Salesforce Agentforce, Oracle Digital Assistant, and custom LLM-based frameworks — covering the full agent lifecycle from strategy and architecture through development, testing, and production operation. We are early adopters of Agentforce Revenue Management and have deployed autonomous agents for lead management, service resolution, CPQ automation, and contract intelligence. Agentforce agent architecture and strategy Custom agent development with low-code and API tools Agent testing with AITest — Forsys's AI-powered QA platform Production deployment, monitoring, and agent optimization The Agentforce Build Framework 01 Assess Map automation opportunities and define agent scope, data requirements, and success metrics 02 Design Architect agent topology, action libraries, data connections, and human-in-the-loop escalation patterns 03 Configure Build agents using Agentforce low-code tools and custom Apex/APIs for complex agent logic 04 Optimize Monitor agent performance, refine prompts, retrain models, and expand agent scope iteratively Key Capabilities Everything you need to build AI agents that work in production Agent Architecture & Strategy Define agent scope, capabilities, data access, action libraries, and escalation triggers — ensuring agents are built on a solid foundation that scales safely across departments and processes. Agentforce Build & Deploy Native Agentforce agent development — configuring Einstein AI actions, Flows, custom Apex tools, and API integrations to build agents that execute across Sales, Service, Revenue, and Commerce clouds without human intervention. Custom Agent Development Build agents beyond the Agentforce toolkit — custom LLM integrations, Oracle AI agents, or multi-agent architectures — using Forsys's AI engineering team and proprietary accelerators. Agent Monitoring & Optimization Post-launch agent performance monitoring — tracking conversation completion rates, escalation volumes, accuracy metrics, and business KPIs — with Forsys's continuous improvement service for deployed agents. text: Configure, Price, Quote (CPQ), the most commonly used sales tool, has been around for decades, but the significance of the software is realized only when companies start growing. The realization comes when the sales team struggles to create accurate quotes on time, frets about the latest pricing rules, misses upselling/ cross-selling, tussles with lengthy negotiations, regrets failed opportunities, and more. Yet, most of the companies continue to use spreadsheets or in-house tools to generate quotes instead of implementing a smart CPQ solution. The reason could be resistance to adoption, fear of unknown/failure, and financial viability. Overcome all the challenges with Forsys’ expertise and learn more about CPQ. Let us explore the solution, its benefits, and how to skirt CPQ implementations failures. Follow usf o r s y s i n c . c o m Know All About CPQ & How to Capitalize on It Benefits What Is Configure, Price, Quote Software, and Why It Is Essential? CPQ is a software solution that automatically generates accurate quotes based on the pre-configured rules. This ensures your sales representatives produce an error-free quote considering the customizations, quantities, applicable discounts, and other pre-programmed guidelines. ARTICLE CPQ Follow usf o r s y s i n c . c o m The solution helps your sales representatives stay focused on engaging with the customer and selling the right products without fretting about the time- consuming administrative tasks to generate accurate proposals/quotes. Your sales reps can access all the information in a few minutes and generate professional-looking documents without wasting any time and enhancing customer experience. The topmost advantage of CPQ solutions is that they build momentum for the sales team. A momentum that accelerates the sales cycle, generates revenue in less time, and spares enough time for the sales reps to focus on the upcoming deals. The top 5 benefits of implementing the CPQ software are: Benefits of CPQ Solutions FASTER SALES - During a deal, a salesperson spends time on value-added tasks like interacting with the customer, exploring the customer’s requirements, proposing solutions, and recommending add-ons. In addition, the salesperson has to do other administrative tasks that consume a lot of time to calculate the pricing. With straightforward and error-free quotes, the sales representatives can save maximum time on administrative tasks, negotiate with the customers effectively, and respond to requests with a few clicks - resulting in faster sales. SWIFT APPROVALS - Cut down the long and complicated chase of getting approvals when the client is ready to make the purchase. Reduce or completely eliminate the approval delays with CPQ. The software triggers automatic notifications for approvals to the assigned authorities along with details of the proposal/ deal. CPQ handles a series of approvals, thereby, providing visibility into the deal’s progress. You can also get insight into the stage where a deal is approved/rejected. These inputs are essential in understanding the sales process better. MAXIMIZE PROFIT MARGINS - Access the latest pricing of your products/ services during a deal with CPQ without any hassle. Even though sales personnel have the authority to provide discounts, access to the right price list is crucial to maintain the company's profitability. A centrally managed CPQ instantly generates the right pricing based on the latest price rules. Even for the most complex configurations, limited offerings, promotions, and other proposals, you can securely get the right pricing while safeguarding the margins. 01 02 03 Follow usf o r s y s i n c . c o m GUIDED SELLING - One of the most-talked-about features of CPQ is Guided Selling where sales representatives follow due process and ask

maintain the company's profitability. A centrally managed CPQ instantly generates the right pricing based on the latest price rules. Even for the most complex configurations, limited offerings, promotions, and other proposals, you can securely get the right pricing while safeguarding the margins. 01 02 03 Follow usf o r s y s i n c . c o m GUIDED SELLING - One of the most-talked-about features of CPQ is Guided Selling where sales representatives follow due process and ask relevant questions to discover customer needs. Guided selling is used when you have an array of products/ services to sell. Based on the response to the questions, the CPQ suggests options that help the sales team make practical suggestions to the customers. In a way, it simplifies the process of selecting the right product. DEALING WITH SUBSCRIPTION MANAGEMENT - Running a subscription- based business model has become easier with the right CPQ tool. Manage your license renewals, calculate subscription pricing, and enhance revenue and profit through cross-selling or up-selling with a few clicks. Sellers can understand the different services used by a customer, know the existing service status, and create new opportunities for selling. 04 05 Apart from these top advantages, other benefits include simplification of operations, dashboards with data analytics on each deal, and increased sales opportunities. If you are looking for the best CPQ vendors in the market, here are the top 4 recommendations: CPQ CPQ CPQ CPQ **Bonus - Tips to Choose the Right CPQ Software Simple to use Seamlessly integrates with other software solutions - CRM, CLM, ERP, et al Suits your business needs Provides data analytics Eases and automates most of the sales process Makes a good financial investment Easily customizable and scalable Does not require additional costs Even with the best CPQ software, if the implementation is not done correctly, companies may not see the desired results. Follow usf o r s y s i n c . c o m A brief prelude on the top reasons why CPQ implementation fails is listed below: Why Do CPQ Implementations Fail? When organizations do not identify and define the objectives of implementing the solution, they stare into the void. Specify the purpose and desired outcome of the software, define the scope of the software (what it needs to do), and list out the time, cost, dependencies, and risks involved for an effective CPQ implementation. The CPQ implementation is an opportunity to thoroughly evaluate your sales processes and provide an effective solution to overcome the challenges. Lack of Clearly Defined Project Goals A pure agile methodology to implement your first CPQ solution does not work best. It is best to choose a hybrid approach while implementing the software. During the design phase, follow the waterfall methodology and thereafter, you can use the agile approach during the development for a greater impact. Incorrect Implementation Methodology Including all the product features merely because they are available may not work for your business. Rather discuss your business requirements with key stakeholders and add only those features that serve your goals. Use the “out-of-box” functionalities first to ensure smooth functioning before customizing the solution. Adding All the Features Partnering with an inexperienced team can adversely affect the implementation process. This is why, collaborating with partners like Forsys can ensure end-to-end support for your Configure, Price, Quote. Inexperienced Implementation Team As CPQ takes data from multiple sources, inaccurate information proves detrimental. Therefore, it is important to determine the correctness of data - pricing rules, discounts, bundles, tax rates, and other information before inputting it into the system. Determining the Right Data

before customizing the solution. Adding All the Features Partnering with an inexperienced team can adversely affect the implementation process. This is why, collaborating with partners like Forsys can ensure end-to-end support for your Configure, Price, Quote. Inexperienced Implementation Team As CPQ takes data from multiple sources, inaccurate information proves detrimental. Therefore, it is important to determine the correctness of data - pricing rules, discounts, bundles, tax rates, and other information before inputting it into the system. Determining the Right Data Quality Adopting a new solution is difficult for any team; and until the sales team approves and assumes the solution in their daily work, the solution fails to deliver. The whole point of the CPQ solution is to support the sales team. Therefore, training and helping teams get acquainted with the new software is decisive in the success of the solution. Our offerings include all the in-between services from assessment to change management ensuring our clients focus on their main business and are confident of the outcome. as Your CPQ Implementation Partner? Salesforce CPQ Beneﬁts 29% Pricing Errors Conﬁguration Errors Time to Generate a Quote Time to Create a Quote Forecast Accuracy Upsell & Cross-sell 21% Quote to Close Time 20%24%29%29%33% Salesforce Billing Beneﬁts Number of days Outstanding 30% 10% Renewal Rates 12% Quote to cash Invoices Disputes 11% For any software product implementation, the launch is one of the most critical parts hands down. Experts would agree that if the implementation goes smoothly, the business can avoid potential problems in the weeks and months that follow. And this holds true for Salesforce CPQ & Billing as well. Companies of all sizes and industries are implementing Salesforce CPQ & Billing to automate and streamline their complete Lead-to-Revenue lifecycle. And report proves that organizations are witnessing real-world benefits. Source: https://www.salesforce.com/content/dam/web/en_us/www/documents/ reports/cpq-billing-ebook-2021.pdf Plan for Salesforce CPQ & Billing Implementation Success Based on our experience of helping global enterprises optimize their mission-critical processes across the entire Lead-to-Revenue cycle by leveraging Salesforce CPQ & Billing, planning is a key factor that drives implementation success. Deploying Salesforce CPQ & Billing quickly without optimizing the processes/data might be tempting, but we strongly advise against it. Reason? Lack of prep-work prior to the implementation makes a project go awry. Also, being unprepared for Salesforce CPQ & Billing leads to implementation delays, error-prone processes, and adoption failure. We understand that embarking on Salesforce CPQ & Billing project can be daunting. Hence, we have gathered implementation best practices from our customer success team that our certified consultants follow to get a complete picture of the initial situation and create a good basis for decision-making. Best Practice #1 Define Project Goals First and foremost, outline the project goals based on the sales process. Understand the sales process needs, define the measurable goals – like less time spent on selling, lower processing time for quotes, increase in order value, precision in quote creation/ generation, more wins, higher renewals, reduced churn – and prioritize goals by order of their criticality to ensure that ROI is achieved. The likelihood of project success increases manifold when the objectives are set, metrics related to the objective are defined, and important business process improvements are identified before deployment kicks off. Ensure that the business requirements list is documented and prioritized - as per critical, high, medium and low - based on the key value drivers for the project. • Reduce overall time spent selling • Improve quote turnaround time • Increase Average Order Value (AOV) • Improve quote accuracy • Reduce churn, increase win rate & renewal rate KPIs to Considered Before Getting Started Best Practice #2 Best Practice #3 Ensure Organizational Readiness Executive/Steering Committee Support Making the switch from disparate, legacy systems onto a centralized platform like Salesforce CPQ & Billing is a major change. Hence, before proceeding with deployment, it is key to ensure the organization (across teams – finance, sales, marketing, legal, etc) is ready to embrace it. Every Salesforce CPQ & Billing project engages multiple stakeholders. It is key to realize that each one of them will need varied types of data and levels of detail. Since the stakeholders will need some time to adjust to the change, offer them a learning curve and emphasize the benefits, bottlenecks, inefficiencies, and backlogs it can resolve. Additionally, engage the stakeholder groups during planning for change management before the project kicks off. Bagging buy-in from the executive leadership/ C-Suite team before starting implementation

& Billing project engages multiple stakeholders. It is key to realize that each one of them will need varied types of data and levels of detail. Since the stakeholders will need some time to adjust to the change, offer them a learning curve and emphasize the benefits, bottlenecks, inefficiencies, and backlogs it can resolve. Additionally, engage the stakeholder groups during planning for change management before the project kicks off. Bagging buy-in from the executive leadership/ C-Suite team before starting implementation ensures direction and support wherever required for a successful rollout. Getting the support of executive champions come in handy to get the time, resources, and financial backing needed to introduce CPQ & Billing. Also, the team doesn’t feel pressured by the implementation process and sets the path for future improvements/upgrades. • Create a single focal point, a “go-to” person, for all the stakeholders to ensure successful preparation, participation and onboarding. • Engage all the stakeholders to plan out the key precesses. • Collaborate with C-Suite to share clear information related to change management. • Keep change management internal. How to Plan Change Management Best Practice #4 Best Practice #5 Be Attentive to Data Management/Migration & System Integration Business Process Optimization To ensure data readiness, focus on what data is needed for the CPQ & Billing process to run and identify the source from which it should be derived. Since it is most likely to be exchanged between the different platforms –ERP and CPQ – it is essential that the data is in real-time, updated, verified, and accurate. Define the source of truth and data owner for each master data (e.g. Customer, Material, Pricing) that will be part of the process. We suggest first collating data related to customers, billing addresses, contacts, products, discount rates, price packages, and template details into the CPQ and then proceeding with integration with ERP/accounting systems, licensing systems, electronic signature applications, and others. It entails identifying a) key business processes, including actors and activities; b) process bottlenecks; c) data consistency; and d) measurable optimization impacts. All the said components are interdependent and crucial for the success of business process optimization. Optimizing the Quote-to-Cash (QTC) process, including processes related to sales, account management, quotes, configuration, pricing, order fulfillment, billing, and accounts receivables functions, is key. We recommend always going through business process optimization before building the systems that support the organizational processes. • ETL tool - to perform extract, transform and load into Salesforce system • Data Loader by Salesforce • Integration - SOAP/REST API • Custom CPQ Accelerator (like FloData) to accelerate migration Data Migration Tools for Salesforce CPQ & Billing Best Practice #6 Prioritize Features Salesforce CPQ & Billing offers a wealth of features that are worth investing in. Having said that, implementing all of them at once can be overwhelming for your team. Considering our experience of working with clients’ complicated business models, we recommend using out-of-the-box functionalities like product catalog, price book, guided selling, product bundles and order management. After the out-of-box features are adopted successfully, adding more capabilities whenever needed should be considered. A well- trained staff will expedite routine tasks by performing smart catalog searches, leveraging guided selling and geo- based sales, resulting in accelerated productivity. Additionally, the CPQ & Billing platform should undergo thorough pre-implementation testing of the functionalities, integrations and workflows by incorporating real-life scenarios. Best Practice #8 Practice Agile Implementation Methodology Geared toward continuous improvement, the agile methodology greatly enhances a project’s prospects for success. According to a report, 98% of businesses experienced success with an agile project. Agile is effective because it uses short development cycles, known as ‘sprints’, to allow continuous progress without implementation hitting roadblocks. This enables course-corrections to be rectified and prioritizes the most important components of the Salesforce CPQ & Billing platform. Additionally, the agile implementation methodology allows for incorporating feedback at all stages, offers a faster response to internal/external alterations, and improves efficiency. Best Practice #10 Best Practice #11 Track Progress Key Performance Metrics to Evaluate Progress Working with an ‘Experienced’ Implementation Partner The same rules don’t work for all businesses. While many times it is necessary to reconfigure the pricing rules or redesign the product models, at other times the pricing rules can follow the ‘trial-and-error’ method. Leverage performance indicators to configure dashboards and reports. For pricing strategies, resort to granular feedback on customer responsiveness. Explore cross-sell opportunities using accurate forecasts. Measure suggestions proactively and filter/choose options that work and bring the desired results. As a rule of thumb, always establish and use KPIs to measure project success/ progress. Additionally, leverage user satisfaction surveys to measure the impactfulness of the platform. Find out how the Salesforce CPQ & Billing solution has helped curtail the efforts of the sales rep to judge its efficiency. The insight can be used to improve the effectiveness and plug the adoption gap. Choosing an implementation partner with solid Sales Cloud expertise but little CPQ and Billing expertise can result in unanticipated process, technical, and change management issues across the Lead-to-Revenue cycle. Organizations considering Salesforce CPQ & Billing implementation should collaborate with an implementation partner with a documented history of planning, implementing, and optimizing technologies across the Lead-to-Revenue lifecycle. Additionally, the implementation partner should have the competency to design and deliver an integrated Lead-to-Revenue platform that supports the Salesforce ecosystem. Hence, before proceeding with deployment, it is key to ensure the organization (across teams – finance, sales, marketing, legal, etc) is ready to embrace it. Every Salesforce CPQ & Billing project engages multiple stakeholders. It is key to realize that each one of them will need varied types of data and levels of detail. Since the stakeholders will need some time to adjust to the change, offer them a learning curve and emphasize the benefits, bottlenecks, inefficiencies, and backlogs it can resolve. Additionally, engage the stakeholder groups during planning for change management before the project kicks off. Bagging buy-in from the executive leadership/ C-Suite team before starting implementation

& Billing project engages multiple stakeholders. It is key to realize that each one of them will need varied types of data and levels of detail. Since the stakeholders will need some time to adjust to the change, offer them a learning curve and emphasize the benefits, bottlenecks, inefficiencies, and backlogs it can resolve. Additionally, engage the stakeholder groups during planning for change management before the project kicks off. Bagging buy-in from the executive leadership/ C-Suite team before starting implementation ensures direction and support wherever required for a successful rollout. Getting the support of executive champions come in handy to get the time, resources, and financial backing needed to introduce CPQ & Billing. Also, the team doesn’t feel pressured by the implementation process and sets the path for future improvements/upgrades. • Create a single focal point, a “go-to” person, for all the stakeholders to ensure successful preparation, participation and onboarding. • Engage all the stakeholders to plan out the key precesses. • Collaborate with C-Suite to share clear information related to change management. • Keep change management internal. How to Plan Change Management Best Practice #4 Best Practice #5 Be Attentive to Data Management/Migration & System Integration Business Process Optimization To ensure data readiness, focus on what data is needed for the CPQ & Billing process to run and identify the source from which it should be derived. Since it is most likely to be exchanged between the different platforms –ERP and CPQ – it is essential that the data is in real-time, updated, verified, and accurate. Define the source of truth and data owner for each master data (e.g. Customer, Material, Pricing) that will be part of the process. We suggest first collating data related to customers, billing addresses, contacts, products, discount rates, price packages, and template details into the CPQ and then proceeding with integration with ERP/accounting systems, licensing systems, electronic signature applications, and others. It entails identifying a) key business processes, including actors and activities; b) process bottlenecks; c) data consistency; and d) measurable optimization impacts. All the said components are interdependent and crucial for the success of business process optimization. Optimizing the Quote-to-Cash (QTC) process, including processes related to sales, account management, quotes, configuration, pricing, order fulfillment, billing, and accounts receivables functions, is key. We recommend always going through business process optimization before building the systems that support the organizational processes. • ETL tool - to perform extract, transform and load into Salesforce system • Data Loader by Salesforce • Integration - SOAP/REST API • Custom CPQ Accelerator (like FloData) to accelerate migration Data Migration Tools for Salesforce CPQ & Billing Best Practice #6 Prioritize Features Salesforce CPQ & Billing offers a wealth of features that are worth investing in. Having said that, implementing all of them at once can be overwhelming for your team. Considering our experience of working with clients’ complicated business models, we recommend using out-of-the-box functionalities like product catalog, price book, guided selling, product bundles and order management. After the out-of-box features are adopted successfully, adding more capabilities whenever needed should be considered. A well- trained staff will expedite routine tasks by performing smart catalog searches, leveraging guided selling and geo- based sales, resulting in accelerated productivity. Additionally, the CPQ & Billing platform should undergo thorough pre-implementation testing of the functionalities, integrations and workflows by incorporating real-life scenarios. Best Practice #8 Practice Agile Implementation Methodology Geared toward continuous improvement, the agile methodology greatly enhances a project’s prospects for success. According to a report, 98% of businesses experienced success with an agile project. Agile is effective because it uses short development cycles, known as ‘sprints’, to allow continuous progress without implementation hitting roadblocks. This enables course-corrections to be rectified and prioritizes the most important components of the Salesforce CPQ & Billing platform. Additionally, the agile implementation methodology allows for incorporating feedback at all stages, offers a faster response to internal/external alterations, and improves efficiency. Best Practice #10 Best Practice #11 Track Progress Key Performance Metrics to Evaluate Progress Working with an ‘Experienced’ Implementation Partner The same rules don’t work for all businesses. While many times it is necessary to reconfigure the pricing rules or redesign the product models, at other times the pricing rules can follow the ‘trial-and-error’ method. Leverage performance indicators to configure dashboards and reports. For pricing strategies, resort to granular feedback on customer responsiveness. Explore cross-sell opportunities using accurate forecasts. Measure suggestions proactively and filter/choose options that work and bring the desired results. As a rule of thumb, always establish and use KPIs to measure project success/ progress. Additionally, leverage user satisfaction surveys to measure the impactfulness of the platform. Find out how the Salesforce CPQ & Billing solution has helped curtail the efforts of the sales rep to judge its efficiency. The insight can be used to improve the effectiveness and plug the adoption gap. Choosing an implementation partner with solid Sales Cloud expertise but little CPQ and Billing expertise can result in unanticipated process, technical, and change management issues across the Lead-to-Revenue cycle. Organizations considering Salesforce CPQ & Billing implementation should collaborate with an implementation partner with a documented history of planning, implementing, and optimizing technologies across the Lead-to-Revenue lifecycle. Additionally, the implementation partner should have the competency to design and deliver an integrated Lead-to-Revenue platform that supports the Salesforce ecosystem. Cash Flow Management for Every Part of Your Business BUSINES S GUIDE It’s no secret that costs have risen dramatically during the past year due to a combination of elevated demand, constrained supply and loose monetary policy. While the U.S. Federal Reserve is taking aggressive action in hopes of controlling inflation, prices won’t feel the impact immediately. It’s too soon to know what long-term impact the Fed’s moves will have on the economy, but one thing is clear: Inflation remains a problem for now. Supply chain issues, the threat of recession and war in Europe will further confound the economy, adding to this period of instability. Grab a seat and enjoy. Read Time: 7 minutes Cash Flow Management for Every Part of Your Business Five problems and five solutions for CFOs now Businesses can experience unexpected cash shortfalls even in the best of times, which is why cash management is always a top concern among financial leaders. Inflation, supply chain snags and labor issues increase the risk of a cash shortfall, especially if a business can’t pass higher costs on to its customers. Throw in ineffective processes, inadequate controls and inaccurate information, and managing cash gets even more difficult – causing otherwise successful companies to struggle. However, these issues are manageable with strong leadership from CFOs and technology to back them up. Page 2© Oracle | Terms of Use and Privacy Table of Contents 2 Reliable Cash Flow Forecasts Page 5 Overview Page 2 1 A Real-Time View of Cash Position Page 4 5 Proactive, Automated Collections Page 8 3 Data-Driven Budgeting Page 6 4 Strong but Flexible Financial Controls Page 7 The problem: Put simply, it’s hard to manage cash well when you don’t know how much you have. Checking a single account balance is easy, but it doesn’t provide a complete picture. The finance team needs to know which transactions have cleared the bank and which haven’t. Does the current balance include the previous day’s deposits? Does it include recent vendor payments? These answers can mean the difference between making or delaying an investment. Getting these answers, however, involves comparing bank transactions to actual accounting entries. Doing this manually is time-consuming, especially with multiple accounts, so some companies only perform bank reconciliation once or twice a month. Basing financial decisions on out-of-date information like this leads to generally poorer results. The solution: Smart cash management decisions require access to real-time financial data, including the current balance across all accounts and a complete record of all bank transactions. A robust accounting system tracks this information and makes it centrally available so that managers work from a common source of data. In NetSuite, the Cash 360 cash management dashboard adds even more insight, monitoring cash flow trends and tracking total cash, accounts receivable and accounts payable balances in real time. A central accounting system also facilities account reconciliation and the application of customer payments to open invoices. When these processes are automated, they can happen more often. In turn, business leaders get a better understanding of their current cash position. Finally, daily and/or real-time bank feeds eliminate the need to enter data manually, saving time and ensuring accuracy. For example, NetSuite’s automated bank feeds import account details nightly and allow for intraday updates, so finance teams know which transactions have cleared and how much cash is available at any time. enter data manually, saving time and ensuring accuracy. For example, NetSuite’s automated bank feeds import account details nightly and allow for intraday updates, so finance teams know which transactions have cleared and how much cash is available at any time. CHAPTER 1 A Real-Time View of Cash Position Page 4© Oracle | Terms of Use and Privacy The problem: Finance leaders must know how much cash the company needs to continue funding the business short-term, as well as the outstanding receivables it is likely to collect. Yet at most organizations, inaccurate cash flow forecasts are the norm. In a survey from APQC, a benchmarking research firm, nearly 60% of respondents indicated that their company’s cash flow forecasts aren’t within 75% of the actual balance. Forecast accuracy will only suffer more as economic headwinds affect suppliers and customers alike. Poor forecast quality begets poor decision-making. Business leaders may become overly conservative if they lack confidence in the numbers, delaying investments in marketing, inventory or other initiatives that could fuel growth. This hesitancy can spark a vicious cycle in which demand falls, stockouts lead to lost sales, and revenue drops – further harming cash flow. Poor forecasting can also lead organizations to request more credit than they need, which typically increases interest rates. The solution: Three factors affect forecast accuracy: the quality of the data used, the validity of the assumptions built into the forecast and the amount of time that has passed since the forecast was updated. To improve accuracy, begin by providing centralized access to real-time financial, sales and other relevant data – ideally in an enterprise resource planning (ERP) system. Next, eliminate unsupported assumptions from the forecasting process. For instance, rather than estimating collections based on instinct or applying a standard percentage to open receivables, companies should use their own historical transaction details. Leaders should also factor in non-operating cash flows including planned capital expenditures, incremental venture investment or other activities. Finally, by automating cash flow forecasts, companies can update them more often, which both improves accuracy and saves time. NetSuite’s Cash 360 dashboard covers all of this, displaying a rolling six-month cash flow forecast based on current transactions and historical collections and disbursement averages. It also considers data points including funding sources, planned expenditures, sales forecasts and billing schedules. CHAPTER 2 Reliable Cash Flow Forecasts Page 5© Oracle | Terms of Use and Privacy CHAPTER 3 Data-Driven Budgeting The problem: In many organizations, the budgeting process begins with desired outcomes, such as a top-line revenue, annual growth rate and/ or earnings target, and then works backward to establish spending limits. Departmental budgets for the coming year tend to mirror the current budget, with slight adjustments to reflect future performance targets. This approach tends to rely more on historical than current facts. And decisions may not consider which expense categories generate the most value or which investments are most likely to improve performance. This process may have worked just fine prior to 2020, but it isn’t sufficient for managing through the inevitable changes in demand and cash flow that come with a changing economy. Inflation, a recession threat and other timely issues can significantly change the assumptions behind budget numbers. If a business doesn’t change its assumptions accordingly, it risks leaving some activities overfunded and others underfunded – and financial performance ultimately suffers. The solution: Instead of establishing a performance target and then building their plans, use a bottom- up budgeting approach that bases plans on data. This will deliver more consistent, predictable results. Begin the process with an assessment of the current business environment and a review of

other timely issues can significantly change the assumptions behind budget numbers. If a business doesn’t change its assumptions accordingly, it risks leaving some activities overfunded and others underfunded – and financial performance ultimately suffers. The solution: Instead of establishing a performance target and then building their plans, use a bottom- up budgeting approach that bases plans on data. This will deliver more consistent, predictable results. Begin the process with an assessment of the current business environment and a review of past initiatives to determine which activities are likely to generate the most value. This gives leaders a better understanding of which results are actually achievable, so they can allocate budgets accordingly. Planning and budgeting capabilities in NetSuite automate the planning process, save time and improve data quality by eliminating the need for cumbersome spreadsheets. They provide sophisticated modeling capabilities, predictive analytics and a powerful calculation engine that allow financial planners to quickly build and evaluate multiple what-if scenarios for more informed, data- driven budgeting decisions. Page 6© Oracle | Terms of Use and Privacy CHAPTER 4 Strong but Flexible Financial Controls The problem: When first starting out, entrepreneurs have complete control of the checkbook, allowing them to personally approve every purchase. As the company grows, the purchasing process evolves, gradually giving more people access to the checkbook or corporate credit card. Controlling expenses becomes more difficult, increasing the risk of excessive or inappropriate spending. To avoid these issues, organizations typically establish formal expense policies and procedures, including spending limits and purchase authorization requirements. These documented processes are only useful if enforced – and too often, they’re not. Many organizations also don’t update them regularly. But just as in budgeting, timely factors like inflation and supply chain issues are changing the fundamental assumptions that inform these controls. So, finance teams need easy ways to assess and change their spending review requirements. The solution: Well-designed internal controls bring greater stability to an organization by holding individuals accountable for their actions. They also assure compliance with government regulations and accounting standards. Many business management solutions help establish this compliance. They build review and approval workflows into processes including accounting, sales, procurement and staffing, providing management oversight that reduces the risk of financial fraud or errors. Effective controls can also improve company performance by ensuring that senior staff sign off on actions from lower-level managers. In NetSuite, configurable workflows in areas like procurement, accounts payable and expenses help companies avoid overspending by enforcing approval policies and holding managers accountable for staying on budget. Embedded controls help save money by automatically logging all user activity, reducing the risk of fraud and maintaining appropriate separation of duties among finance and accounting staff. Page 7© Oracle | Terms of Use and Privacy CHAPTER 5 Proactive, Automated Collections The problem: In an ideal world, customers always pay on time and in full. In reality, companies frequently have delinquent accounts. Collections efforts often fall to accounts receivable staff, who perform them ad hoc and focus mainly on accounts that are long past due, making it more difficult to secure payment. And the tougher the economic conditions, the more likely it is that some customers will pay late or need to renegotiate payment terms. In a difficult business environment, customers who once paid reliably may run into trouble. It’s hard to assess how many customers – and which specific ones – will have trouble paying. The solution: Use a business management system to establish an effective collections process that keeps days sales outstanding (DSO) under control. These systems can automate customer communications to ensure consistent follow up on past-due invoices

it is that some customers will pay late or need to renegotiate payment terms. In a difficult business environment, customers who once paid reliably may run into trouble. It’s hard to assess how many customers – and which specific ones – will have trouble paying. The solution: Use a business management system to establish an effective collections process that keeps days sales outstanding (DSO) under control. These systems can automate customer communications to ensure consistent follow up on past-due invoices and reduce bad debt write-offs. Automation can even improve on-time payment by alerting customers of pending due dates. For example, NetSuite’s automated dunning letters solution improves on-time payment by alerting customers before bills are due. It also helps avoid write-offs for bad debt by maintaining consistent, proactive communications with delinquent accounts. See how your business can make smarter cash flow decisions with NetSuite. Resulted in faster deal closure by ~34%. Improved collaboration between different stakeholders and operations teams. Contents 1.0 Introduction 2 2.0 Understanding Types of Upgrades 2 3.0 Technical Upgrade Process 33.1 Upgrade Presentation 33.2 Customer Engagement 3Discovery Questionnaire 33.3 Technical Upgrade Assessment 43.3.1 Forsys Assessment Matrix 43.3.2 Customer Readiness 53.3.3 Estimate Timeline 63.3.4 Scheduling Upgrade 63.3.5 Managing Upgrade 6 4.0 Functional Upgrade Process 6 5.0 Tools 7 6.0 Testing Automation 7 7.0 Roles & Responsibilities 7 8.0 Best Practices 7 9.0 References 8 1 1.0 Introduction Conga is a comprehensive Quote-to-Cash (QTC) solution helping customers automateQuoting/Proposal, Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM), Billing, Order Management & RevenueManagement processes. Conga is built native on Salesforce. Customers purchase Conga packages to automate their business processes, enable and enhancebusiness models, and grow their revenue. They choose module (s) based on the business needand that are suitable for their line of business. They also hire a System Integrator to set up,conﬁgure and customize these application packages for their business needs. System Integrators follow a standard software implementation process & methodologies toconﬁgure the system to customer needs: ● Discover existing (AS-IS) customer-speciﬁc business processes● Identify and deﬁne future state business processes (TO-BE)● Document detailed requirements● Analyze and design the system to meet their future state requirements● Identify the gaps between what package supports and what customer is envisioning afuture state● Identify the list of customizations that need to be performed on the standard applicationpackages● Build the system – conﬁgure, customize, and integrate● Test the systems thoroughly to validate & ensure that the systems meet customerrequirements● Train the users on the new system● Get the system ready for users The following playbook is created based on: ● Best practices for performing managed package upgrades on Salesforce● Forsys’ experiences in upgrading Conga and related packages for several medium/largecustomers forConﬁgure-Price-Quote(CPQ), CLM, Billing, Ecommerce & RevenueManagement 2.0 Understanding Types of Upgrades Salesforce managed package upgrades can be classiﬁed as (a) Technical and (b) Functional.Technical Upgrades will ensure customers are using the latest version of the managed packages(including objects, application code, etc). This will help customers to have the latest versions ofthe software installed on Salesforce. Salesforce highlyrecommends all its customers upgradetheir packages to the latest versions to minimize their support and maintenance costs. 2 Functional Upgrades will ensure customers are leveraging the latest features and functionalityincorporated into the software to support additional business processes. This follows thestandard implementation process & methodology (asdescribed above). Additionally, this processinvolves conducting detailed gap analysis, reversing customizations, implementing new featuresand functionality as required to support their business. 3.0 Technical Upgrade Process Review the install-base to identify the list of customers who do not have the latest version of theConga packages. Document the list of modules that customers installed and their versions. 3.1 Upgrade Presentation Prepare a customer presentation detailing the list of new features and functionality included ineach of the modules (refer to the release notes or product documentation for the release). Includethe beneﬁts and justiﬁcation to upgrade the packages. Document the high-level upgrade processand estimated timeline. 3.2 Customer Engagement Identify the customers who have Conga packages older than two releases. Identify its CongaAccount Manager or Conga Customer Success Manager. Initiate a call with the customer andshare the details of the latest release, justify the urgency to upgrade to the latest version andhigh-level process for the upgrade. Also, share the discovery questionnaire with the customer. Discovery Questionnaire Share the discovery questionnaire (referred to as Forsys-Upgrade-Assessment-Matrix) with thecustomer and schedule a session to review their responses. ● The key point of contact from Business or IT who is managing/supporting the businessoperations● List of modules implemented● Timelines of go-live● List of customizations● Changes since go-live● List of test scenarios● Last upgrade – any documentation

release, justify the urgency to upgrade to the latest version andhigh-level process for the upgrade. Also, share the discovery questionnaire with the customer. Discovery Questionnaire Share the discovery questionnaire (referred to as Forsys-Upgrade-Assessment-Matrix) with thecustomer and schedule a session to review their responses. ● The key point of contact from Business or IT who is managing/supporting the businessoperations● List of modules implemented● Timelines of go-live● List of customizations● Changes since go-live● List of test scenarios● Last upgrade – any documentation and key errors post-upgrade● List of issues or enhancement requirements from Business● Changes to product & pricing models since go-live 3 3.3 Technical Upgrade Assessment The Technical Upgrade will ensure the customer will have the latest Conga packages installed.Anyhow, a careful assessment is needed to estimate the test coverage when the packages areupgraded. So, start with a primary discovery questionnaire to gather basic details from thecustomer and then ﬁll in the assessment matrix that includes the features & functionality used bythe customer and the extent of customizations done to base modules. 3.3.1 Forsys Assessment Matrix Upon the completion of responses to the discovery questionnaire, determine the testing coverageand complexity based on the following key metrics provided by the customer. Low Medium HighBusiness Flows New & Renewal;Add Ons Swaps, Upgrades,Addons Custom Flows, PartnerQuotingNumber of Regions 1 (ex: US) <=2 (ex: US, Canada) >2Product Lines <=5 <=10 >10Product Attributes <=25 <50 >50Product Rules <25 <75 >75Bundle levels 1 <=2 >2Pricing Models 2 (ex: per unit,recurring) <=4 (ex: per unit,tiered, usage, service) >4 Pricelists <=2 <=5 >5Number of Currencies 1 <=3 >3Approval Processes 1 <=2 >2Number of ProposalTemplates <=2 <=4 >4 Proposal OutputLanguage 1 <=2 >3 Reporting Base reports Few reportsCustomized Many reportscustomizedPartner Quoting Not Implemented Base functionality CustomizedECommerce Not Implemented Not Implemented ImplementedIntegration Not Integratedwith ERP Order Integrationwith ERP Integrated with severalboundary systemsDate of the lastupgrade Older by 2releases Older by 3 to 5releases Older by 5+ releases Customizations(Conﬁguration, Pricing,Workﬂow, Templates,Approvals, Others) NoCustomizations MinimalCustomizations (lessthan 2 areas) More than 2 areas 4 3.3.2 Customer Readiness Customer readiness is a critical factor for the technical upgrade process. It is usually categorizedas High, Medium & Low. If the customer has all thetesting scenarios, test scripts and test datadocumented, then it substantially reduces the upgrade timelines. High ● The Solution Design Documents (along with the list of customizations) are updated to thelatest changes to the production environment● Test scenarios, Test scripts & Test data is the latest and is available● No dependent project is in progress that impacts the upgrade● Testing environments are available for Quality Assurance (QA) andUser AcceptanceTesting (UAT).● A dedicated team available to complete the test scenarios● One Point of Contact from Customer Medium ● The Solution Design Documents (along with the list of customizations) are not updated tothe latest changes to the production environment; the customer needs additional time todetermine the latest changes to the production environment● Test scenarios, Test scripts & Test data is not the latest; the customer needs some time todetermine/document the latest changes to the production● A dependent project is in progress that impacts the upgrade; additional analysis is neededto assess the impact of the upgrade● Testing environments are not available for QA and UAT immediately● A dedicated team is not available to complete the test scenarios; the customer COE teamcan document the test scenarios for Forsys resources to complete testing● One Point of Contact from Customer Low ● The Solution Design Documents (along with the list of customizations) are not available● Test scenarios, Test scripts & Test data are not available● Multiple dependent projects are in progress that impacts the upgrade● Need to request for provisioning

environments are not available for QA and UAT immediately● A dedicated team is not available to complete the test scenarios; the customer COE teamcan document the test scenarios for Forsys resources to complete testing● One Point of Contact from Customer Low ● The Solution Design Documents (along with the list of customizations) are not available● Test scenarios, Test scripts & Test data are not available● Multiple dependent projects are in progress that impacts the upgrade● Need to request for provisioning the testing environments● Current resources are busy; resources need to be identiﬁed (from Forsys or anotherpartner) to complete the test scenarios● One Point of Contact from Customer 5 3.3.3 Estimated Timeline Upgrade timelines are based on the complexity of the implementation (refer to assessmentmatrix) and customer readiness. The following matrix shows high-level estimates for the timetaken to complete the technical upgrade process. Implementation ComplexityLow Medium High Customer Readiness High 2 Weeks 4 Weeks 6 WeeksMedium 4 Weeks 6 Weeks 8 WeeksLow 6 Weeks 9 Weeks 12 Weeks 3.3.4 Scheduling Upgrade After the timeline is determined and agreed by the customer: ● mobilize & identify resources on both sides● publish the Upgrade plan● validate checklists● Upgrade packages and test thoroughly● conduct UAT to gather business sign-offs● deploy in a production environment 3.3.5 Managing Upgrade Refer to Congareference documentationfor detailedupgrade instructions. Forsys will provide anCONGA Upgrade Documentation (based on analysis, observations, test results, changes,etc) 4.0 Functional Upgrade Process The initial customer engagement process is similar to the technical upgrade. Anyhow, theFunctional Upgrade process involves detailed gap analysis that involves: ● discovery of customizations● new features and functionality● de-customization● implementation plan, design, development, testing and deployment The schedule and timelines of the functional upgrades are determined based on the discoveryand gap analysis. 6 5.0 Tools Forsys is in the process of developing assessment tools that determine the complexity of theimplementation. 6.0 Testing Automation Testing is the most critical factor for the upgrade projects; detailed regression suits are availablefor standard (out of the box) features & functionality. Anyhow, these test suits need to beconﬁgured based on the test scenarios, test data and level of complexity of the customerimplementation. 7.0 Roles & Responsibilities The following roles and responsibilities are involved in the Upgrade projects. Customer Roles Conga Roles Forsys Roles ● Project Manager● Business Analyst● Quality Analysts(optional) ● Customer Engagementteam● Customer SuccessManager / CustomerSupport Partner(optional)● Account Manager(optional) ● Business Analyst● Conga Developer● Quality Analyst (optional)● Test Automation Engineer(optional) 8.0 Best Practices Forsys highly recommends customers against customizing packages and leveraging the features& functionality supported in the package. Customization is recommended only when a feature orfunctionality is not available in the package. Anyhow, the following are the key learnings and bestpractices for Conga Upgrades. ● Need a single Point of Contact (POC)from the customer(either from IT or from BusinessOperations)● Full Sandbox environment for upgrade & testing.● POC to review Release Documentation and discuss the known issues with the customerand their impact● Customer to provide Documentation for: 7 o solution design for the original implementation and subsequent changeso list of customizationso test scenarios, cases and data (mandatory)● Automation Testing Strategyo Automation for standard regression test scenarioso Manual Testing anywhere the rules and expressions are involved; anythingcustomized● Demos and UAT from the customer● Identiﬁcation of pain pointso Support cases / Critical issues● Gap analysis for Functional Upgradeso Identify customized features that need to be rolled backo Identify new features that improve the user experience or address a pain point● Product-related issues need to be reviewed by the Conga customer support team 9.0 References ● Conga Release Notes and Upgrade documentation● Installed Conga Packages 8

The Background The Challenges The client is a leading manufacturer of ice chests, drink containers, and supporting accessories since 1947. The Texas-based globally recognized manufacturer through its 500+ products offerings have played a crucial role in making the family outdoor recreation activities possible and feasible. The client wanted to integrate multiple applications into a single cloud-based enterprise architecture that enabled business growth through a digital transformation and supported its efforts to boost operational excellence. Dealing with operational challenges was one of the key factors that compelled the client to identify a technology solution that aligns with the business objectives and can push the company’s growth forecast. The manufacturing giant came to realize it was time to replace the traditional business methods with technology-enabled business operations to speed up the transition to a next-gen technology-focused enterprise capable of navigating the complexities. Understanding that a phased approach will work best, the client’s main concern was to streamline the demand management process and the sales and operations planning in the first phase and go with the constrained supply plan in the second phase. With over 500 products sold across hundreds of retailers around the globe, the client’s decision to integrate multiple applications into a single cloud-based enterprise architecture was based on the below-mentioned challenges: ▶ Constantly growing demand patterns and lack of ability to forecast demand with greater accuracy. ▶ Heightened risks of supply disruption and lack of ability to identify risks and get rid of them. ▶ Unable to embrace and leverage varied fulfilment strategies within a single global supply chain plan. ▶ Absence of an integrated view of demand and supply across the chain. Forsys streamlines demand management, sales & operations planning, and constrained supply with Oracle Planning Central Cloud for a global cooler manufacturer About The Client Case Study forsysinc.com Follow us The Solution Benefits & Results Keen to address several key operational challenges, the client decided to deploy Planning Central to streamline and make improvements in demand planning, sales and operations planning and more. Moreover, it also wanted to move Planning Central to the cloud while retaining business transactions in Oracle E-Business Suite (EBS). To realize value quickly and consistently for the client, the Forsys team developed a plan to implement the Planning Central leveraging their two-phased approach. Forsys used Flodata, a proprietary integration tool to connect the cloud and on-premise applications and integrate the data using File-Based Data Import (FBDI) templates. In this process, we mapped the attributes in the required FBDI templates from the Source EBS system. Flodata collected the master data and transaction data from the EBS source and loaded it into the Oracle Planning Central Cloud. With seamless data flow between the EBS system and Planning Central, the implementation process was simplified and accelerated. Forsys’ methodical approach ensured that the client’s key objectives were achieved, enabling the business to run in the most optimized manner. The manufacturing giant was satisfied that it selected Forsys for it helped to achieve the below-mentioned results: ▶ Automation helped save time by 250%. ▶ Accurate forecasting led to on-time delivery and boosted the revenue stream. ▶ Optimized inventory management leading to $1 million in savings. ▶ Successful integrated supply chain planning and demand forecasting on the cloud. ▶ Find out the demand-supply balance of the stock keeping units leveraging the daily plans and simulation in real-time. ▶ Reduced end-to-end planning cycle time and planning frequency to quickly address demand variability. ▶ Boosted supply chain data accuracy and curtailed data latency. ▶ Reduced order-rescheduling instances & enhanced forecasting accuracy based on supply chain inventory. forsysinc.com Follow us Supply Chain Planning

Insight The US-based client is a printing technology company facing challenges in subscription modules. The client was manually modifying its subscription lines, which slowed down the invoice processing. Forsys used the oracle order management solution to automate the billing process. As a result, the client observed 100% accurate reconciliation, visibility into real-time revenue data, 95% reduction in manual efforts and more. Business Challenges Forsys’ Oracle Order Management Solution Results in 100% Accurate Reconciliation, Provides Real-time Visibility in Revenue The client was facing multiple challenges in subscription modules and were looking for solutions that could automatically generate invoices with accurate pricing and consumption over a defined period. The key challenges were: • Manual modification of multiple subscription lines based on recurring billing plans. For Ex- if the subscription is for 5 years with monthly recurring billing, the user had to enter 60 lines and they had to calculate monthly billing amount. • Generation of Subsequent Invoices based on Recurring Billing Plan. • Revision or putting multiple lines on hold for the subscription line until the customer acceptance. • Cancellation of subscription in an on-going tenure or before the original tenure expires. • Lack of real-time visibility into billing and financial activities. Case Study Industry: Manufacturing Revenue: $100+ M Employees: 201-500 Headquarters: California, USA Follow us forsysinc.com Follow us Transformation Journey Forsys provided different Order Flows (covered item, subscription flow, covered and subscription flow, and cancellation of the subscription flow) to address the various business needs of the customer. As a part of the solution, Forsys automated the clients’ billing cycles and provided flexibility to structure payment plans based on the client’s requirements within the system. The client can track and view its revenue in real-time and make changes on the go. By creating a more unified and simplified user journey, Forsys helped the manufacturing company focus on its core business strategy and execution. The transformation journey included: • Creation of One invoice instead of multiple invoices. • Generation of invoice begins from the contract start date till the acceptance date. Thereafter, a monthly invoice is generated. • Billing starts only after user acceptance. • Generation of credit memos on a pro-rata basis on cancellation. • Simplification of revision rates as the invoices are generated as per the customer’s choice. • Creation of future invoices based on the revised dates. The impact of the Oracle Order Management solution: Impact ~70% time saved in manual data entries 100% accuracy through automated subscription processes 90% reduction in user efforts by automating recurring invoices 100% accurate and seamless reconciliation Simplified Subscription flows Set up automatic renewals to streamline recurring subscription billing Prevention of revenue leakage Complete visibility into the revenue forecast Enabled pro-rated subscription cancellation in an ongoing tenure and generated automatic credit memos About the Client The client is a venture-backed 3D printing technology company based in California. The company manufactures solutions that help its customers develop distinguished products and reach the market in less time. Global organizations use the client’s digital manufacturing platform to create a wide range of functional end-use parts and print them reliably as required.
