iPaaS vs RPA: Whitepaper Understand the Nuances in Modern Automation Approaches forsysinc.com | Follow us In an era marked by digital dynamism, businesses are steering towards modernization to accelerate their operational efficiencies. Among the myriad of technologies powering this digital revolution, Integration Platform as a Service (iPaaS) and Robotic Process Automation (RPA) stand out as primary game-changers. Although both are automation tools, they offer distinctly different pathways to transforming business operations. This comprehensive whitepaper will delineate the unique attributes of iPaaS and RPA, shedding light on their differentiated applications and potential for driving business automation. Introduction forsysinc.com Follow us iPaaS iPaaS operates as a cloud-based service that fuses disparate applications into a unified, cohesive system. Its primary function is to facilitate the integration of on-premises and cloud-based applications, thereby allowing seamless data exchange and collaboration. As a centralized integration hub, iPaaS effectively bridges the digital divide within and between organizations. iPaaS: The Integration Maestro The global iPaaS market is poised to hit USD 48.80 million by 2030, with a prediction to grow at a CAGR of 33.35% from 2023 to 2030 66% of businesses plan to invest in iPaaS for data integration and automation, while 27% have already done so iPaaS in Numbers forsysinc.com | Follow us forsysinc.com Follow us RPA: The Task Automator RPA is engineered to emulate human behavior, taking over repetitive, rule- based tasks that plague various business processes. It interacts with existing software applications like a human user, eliminating the necessity for intensive changes to the existing IT infrastructure. The global RPA market is projected to reach USD 50.50 billion by 2030 RPA has boosted productivity in 95% of the organizations utilizing it RPA in Numbers The Automation Matrix iPaaS and RPA both aim for automation but differ in their methods. iPaaS uses a system-centered approach for extensive data integration, while RPA emphasizes individual task automation, often as an interim solution for process inefficiencies. The Scope of Automation iPaaS is favored for intricate integration projects dealing with diverse systems and large data sets, making it ideal for organizations aiming for seamless data flow and system interoperability. Conversely, RPA shines in smaller automation tasks, such as streamlining data entry, invoicing, and other administrative functions. Deployment Complexity and Speed RPA's speedy deployment bypasses root problems, while iPaaS, though complex to implement, sets a durable, scalable automation base for long- term efficiency and adaptability. System Interoperability iPaaS promotes system interoperability, allowing different software applications to communicate and work together. RPA, on the other hand, works within existing systems to automate tasks. Scalability iPaaS solutions are generally more scalable than RPA, designed to handle increased data volume and system connections as your business grows. Return on Investment While both iPaaS and RPA can provide cost savings, the return on investment for iPaaS tends to be higher in the long run due to its comprehensive impact on operational efficiency. iPaaS and RPA: The Distinguishing Factors forsysinc.com Follow us forsysinc.com Follow us Choose Wisely: iPaaS or RPA for Your Automation Journey iPaaS RPA iPaaS and RPA each bring unique automation qualities to the table, differentiated by their functionalities, complexities, costs, and security aspects. In a setup dominated by legacy applications lacking API support, RPA could be an ideal choice. Conversely, for situations where API-centric applications and databases prevail, iPaaS is tailored to facilitate durable, manageable, and API- centric integrations. In response, we offer solution accelerators as a subscription model, specifically tailored to confront these issues. But FloData's ambition extends

Client Background The client had no formal product life cycle management and software development life cycle. In addition, they were not able to document and analyze defects, test applications and maintain standards. As a result, it was difficult to measure the overall effectiveness of testing and, in effect, the software quality. The client was looking to establish a quality assurance (QA) structure and process that is capable of meeting the industry requirements. NextGen Testing powered by Automation Solution Forys used a global delivery model with onsite and offshore. While one resource is still available at the client site, we still implement and manage the QA project from offshore. We established industry-wide best practices in documentation and QA cycle management. We developed high-level test scenarios and mapped them to test cases, while ensuring that the high level test scenarios are reviewed and signed off from customers in the design phase. Our Business Driven QA approach helped us to identify critical business test scenarios in advance. We measured the testing coverage provided by these high level scenarios and detailed our findings in a report at the end of each testing cycle. We executed 200 test cases and identified 40 critical errors. We implemented the new QA process for one complete software test life cycle (STLC) for the client. Technical Challenges During the course of the project, Forsys faced the following challenges: • Automation tool selection - The challenge was to select the GUI automation testing tool and software quality assurance and governance tool. After careful assessment, we recommended Selenium which is Free and Open source tool. We also assessed the potential future risks that the client might encounter by using these tools. • Selenium tool benefits • Ease of implementation • Multi OS/Multi Browser Supports • Parallel Test execution • Integration with other tools like Test Management, CICD, Defect Tracking etc. • Customized automation framework • Screen capture and Flexibility • Easy to read logs and debug • HTML, Customized Reports • Easy maintenance Tool compatibility Challenges were encountered in determining if JIRA (Bug Tracking Tool) and Test Management Tool were compatible. Benefits After implementing Forsys’s recommendations for its QA process, the client had the following benefits: • Established a formal product lifecycle management and software development life cycle. • Improved the product quality through a QA process and wider coverage of testing areas • Achieved faster time to market and quicker ROI along with assured quality results through Forsys customized selenium Quality automation framework (QAF) • Reduced the test cycle time by 20-25% by leveraging Forsys Quality CoE automation framework • Established industry standard QA practices.

The Background The Challenges Harmonic is a US-based worldwide leader in Video Delivery Technology solutions and the first to provide broadcasters, pay-TV operators, service providers, and other media companies with operational video efficiency that is ready to deploy on a global scale. Incorporated over 25 years ago, it helps customers respond to the challenges of the Digital Video revolution while reaping its benefits, including the ability to deliver stunning video to viewers on any screen, anywhere. The American technology major was running its applications on Oracle E-Business Suite (EBS) R11, 9i database which started to display signs of distress regarding performance, reliability and resilience. Being in the end-of- life mode, the necessary updates and support were not available for Oracle R11 which made the maintenance difficult. With time, operational efficiency started to impact Harmonic’s productivity of teams. Seeking to address several key operational challenges, Harmonic chose to move its operations from legacy Oracle EBS to Oracle Cloud. Oracle Cloud offers many benefits, but the following five reasons were the deciding factors in finalizing the decision to migrate: ▶ Leverage advanced features of Oracle Cloud for better performance and integrations. ▶ Modernize applications to fully transform and move towards the digital future. ▶ Enhance data integrity to 100% by moving legacy systems to Oracle Cloud. ▶ Refine data reconciliations for month-end/quarter-end closings which typically required 4-5 days. ▶ Improve efficiency and visibility through a single dashboard for Finance Managers and CFOs. Harmonic was looking for a comprehensive solution to replace the ageing software and to be able to leverage modern technology for performance gains. The tech giant came to realize that it needed a single tool that can integrate multiple systems to resolve the latency issues caused by the ineffectual integrations. The varied challenges that plagued Harmonic were: ▶ Lack of a single tool for integrations across applications. ▶ Absence of tools/connectors for integrating Oracle EBS and Salesforce with Oracle Cloud Platform. ▶ Varied data structures and formats of their existing orders (master and transactional data) made data migration difficult. Learn how Forsys helped Harmonic seamlessly migrate data from legacy systems to Oracle Cloud and improve their user experience About The Client Case Study forsysinc.com Follow us The Solution Benefits & Results Harmonic engaged Forsys to lead this transformation, given our deep expertise in Oracle Cloud Migrations and understanding of its technology and business operations. Forsys was tasked to understand Harmonic’s legacy system and recommend how it can be migrated to Oracle Cloud without any business disruptions. We took stock of the situation for Harmonic and helped the technology company to accelerate back on track and migrate its legacy systems to a modern Oracle cloud platform. As a first step, Forsys applied cloud assessment and transformation framework for a combined upgrade and migration to Oracle Cloud. Forsys leveraged Flodata to connect the Cloud and On-Premise applications and integrate Oracle Cloud, Salesforce, and Agile Systems. Flodata facilitated event-driven integration, maximizing the potential of cloud-native applications for real-time analytics and decision making. Flodata’s reconciliation feature ensured data and system integrity for business processes by validating and reconciling data right at the entry- point. Using the Oracle Cloud connectors from Flodata, Forsys successfully migrated Harmonic’s transactional and master data to the Cloud, reducing the schedule and cost by 40%. text: The Impact of NetSuiteCapabilities in EdTech Shaping Educational Dynamics: WHITEPAPER Introduction The Education Technology (EdTech) industry represents a dynamic intersection where technology meets education, aiming to revolutionize the traditional paradigms of teaching and learning. At its core, EdTech encompasses a wide range of software, hardware, and technological processes designed to facilitate, enhance, and personalize education. This industry has become increasingly crucial in today’s rapidly evolving digital landscape, fundamentally transforming how educators teach and students learn. | Follow usforsysinc.com The Role of Technology in Transforming Education Technology is reshaping the educational landscape in profound ways, offering unprecedented opportunities and solutions to longstanding challenges such as classroom teaching, one-size-fits-all learning model, etc. With digital platforms, education is more accessible than ever before. Online courses, e-learning portals, and educational apps allow learners from all over the world to access quality education, irrespective of their geographical location. In addition, technology facilitates personalized education, catering to the individual learning styles, speeds, and needs of students. Student learning technologies that tailor content and pace based on individual performance are a significant step towards a more personalized learning experience. Also, the use of interactive tools and multimedia content has made learning more engaging. Technologies like Virtual and Augmented Reality (VR/AR) are creating immersive and interactive educational experiences, making complex concepts easier to grasp. In this paper, let us explore the role and impact of NetSuite in the EdTech sector and look at a case study on a startup in the industry. | Follow usforsysinc.com Personalized Learning: One of the most significant trends is the shift towards personalized learning. EdTech is enabling the customization of education to suit individual learning styles and paces, using adaptive learning technologies and AI-driven platforms. According to research, the market size of adaptive learning is forecasted to be $9.11 billion by 2028. Increased Use of AI and Machine Learning: AI and machine learning are being increasingly used for predictive analytics in student performance, automating administrative tasks, and even in creating personalized learning plans. Gamification: Integrating game mechanics into the learning process to enhance student engagement and motivation has become a popular trend. This includes the use of points, badges, and leaderboards to make learning more interactive and enjoyable. The impact of gamified learning is 13% according to a guide on top 10 education technology in 2023. VR/AR Learning: These technologies are being used to create immersive learning experiences, making complex subjects more accessible and engaging through virtual simulations and interactive environments. According to this guide, the impact of immersive learning is 22% in 2023. Growth of Online Learning: Platforms offering Massive Open Online Courses, webinars, and virtual classrooms have become more prevalent, indicating a permanent shift in how education is delivered. Focus on STEAM Education: There's a growing emphasis on Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, and Mathematics education, with many EdTech products focusing on these areas to prepare students for future careers. Overview of the EdTech Sector: Current Trends, Challenges, and Opportunities Current Trends in the EdTech Sector The EdTech industry is currently experiencing a wave of innovative trends, facing distinct challenges, and embracing unique opportunities. Let's delve into each of these aspects to understand the current state and future potential of this dynamic sector. | Follow usforsysinc.com | Follow usforsysinc.com Data Privacy and Security: As EdTech relies heavily on data, ensuring student privacy and data security is a critical challenge, requiring strict compliance with regulations like GDPR and COPPA. Ensuring Quality and Effectiveness: With the rapid proliferation of EdTech tools and resources, ensuring their educational quality and effectiveness remains a challenge. There’s a need for standardization and evaluation frameworks. Integration with Traditional Education

understand the current state and future potential of this dynamic sector. | Follow usforsysinc.com | Follow usforsysinc.com Data Privacy and Security: As EdTech relies heavily on data, ensuring student privacy and data security is a critical challenge, requiring strict compliance with regulations like GDPR and COPPA. Ensuring Quality and Effectiveness: With the rapid proliferation of EdTech tools and resources, ensuring their educational quality and effectiveness remains a challenge. There’s a need for standardization and evaluation frameworks. Integration with Traditional Education Systems: Integrating EdTech into existing education systems and curriculums can be challenging, often requiring significant changes in teaching methods and infrastructure. The Background The Challenges The US-based worldwide leader in Video Delivery Technology solutions and the first to provide broadcasters, pay-TV operators, service providers, and other media companies with operational video efficiency that is ready to deploy on a global scale. Incorporated over 25 years ago, it helps customers respond to the challenges of the Digital Video revolution while reaping its benefits, including the ability to deliver stunning video to viewers on any screen, anywhere. The American technology major was running its applications on Oracle E-Business Suite (EBS) R11, 9i database which started to display signs of distress regarding performance, reliability and resilience. Being in the end-of- life mode, the necessary updates and support were not available for Oracle R11 which made the maintenance difficult. With time, operational efficiency started to impact the customer’s team productivity. Seeking to address several key operational challenges, the client chose to move its operations from legacy Oracle EBS to Oracle Cloud. Oracle Cloud offers many benefits, but the following five reasons were the deciding factors in finalizing the decision to migrate: ▶ Leverage advanced features of Oracle Cloud for better performance and integrations. ▶ Modernize applications to fully transform and move towards the digital future. ▶ Enhance data integrity to 100% by moving legacy systems to Oracle Cloud. ▶ Refine data reconciliations for month-end/quarter-end closings which typically required 4-5 days. ▶ Improve efficiency and visibility through a single dashboard for Finance Managers and CFOs. The client was looking for a comprehensive solution to replace the ageing software and to be able to leverage modern technology for performance gains. The tech giant came to realize that it needed a single tool that can integrate multiple systems to resolve the latency issues caused by the ineffectual integrations. The varied challenges that plagued the tech company were: ▶ Lack of a single tool for integrations across applications. ▶ Absence of tools/connectors for integrating Oracle EBS and Salesforce with Oracle Cloud Platform. ▶ Varied data structures and formats of their existing orders (master and transactional data) made data migration difficult. Forsys was tasked to understand the client’s legacy system and recommend how it can be migrated to Oracle Cloud without any business disruptions. We took stock of the situation for the client and helped the technology company to accelerate back on track and migrate its legacy systems to a modern Oracle cloud platform. As a first step, Forsys applied cloud assessment and transformation framework for a combined upgrade and migration to Oracle Cloud. Forsys leveraged Flodata to connect the Cloud and On-Premise applications and integrate Oracle Cloud, Salesforce, and Agile Systems. Flodata’s reconciliation feature ensured data and system integrity for business processes by validating and reconciling data right at the entry- point. The Background The Challenges The San Jose-based client is a worldwide leader in video delivery technology solutions and enables media companies and service providers to deliver ultra-high-quality broadcast and OTT video services to consumers globally. Incorporated over 25 years ago, the client revolutionized cable access networking via the industry’s first virtualized CCAP solution, enabling cable operators to more flexibly deploy gigabit internet service to consumers’ homes and mobile devices. forsysinc.com Follow us The video technology giant moved to the Oracle On-premise (Finance, SCM, CRM) more than two decades back. Since then, there have been multiple changes in their processes and best practices. The legacy applications had grown in size and complexity leading to higher infrastructure cost and hampering performance. The client was desperately looking for ways to curtail cost and modernize their infrastructure for reliability, scalability, flexibility and performance to meet future organizational demands. Before undertaking the migration of their mission-critical applications and processes to Oracle Cloud Suite, the client wanted to evaluate its capabilities and suitability to ensure successful and seamless migration. This was why our client engaged with multiple vendors to identify a futuristic and reliable solution provider capable of modernizing their efforts to make the switch to Oracle Cloud efficient. The client had been rapidly growing with record new bookings, sequential, quarterly and year-over-year revenue growth. Despite recognizing the need for Cloud migration, the client remained wary about transitioning to the Oracle Cloud. It wanted to be fully sure that it will be able to leverage all of its preferred applications uninterrupted. The absence of Cloud proved to be a technical nightmare for the client and led to the below- mentioned challenges: ▶ Inconsistent parent and child account relationships (for example, one child account comprising multiple parent accounts) impacted the chart of accounts hierarchy. ▶ Monthly/quarterly end process was taking over 4 days to complete. ▶ Current application couldn’t fully support intercompany transactions for cross charges. ▶ Manual handling of the statutory requirement of France to meet Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP). ▶ Higher depreciation cost compared to the actual cost for a few assets. Global video delivery technology conglomerate uses Forsys to handle its Oracle Cloud Suite migration confidently About The Client Case Study The Solution Benefits & Results The client wanted to migrate to Oracle Cloud Suite to optimize their operations and to transform Finance, SCM, HR and CRM processes. After evaluating a couple of different solution providers, the client remained frustrated with the level of inflexibility they continued to encounter. On top of it, none of them provided a proof of concept (POC). That is until they found Forsys. Our unique expertise in the Oracle Cloud space and 20+ years of experience handling multiple Oracle Cloud Projects impressed the client. Forsys provided an Oracle Cloud proof of concept (POC) to understand their current landscape and expectations to deliver a seamless experience. Forsys’ Cloud experts defined the business requirements, technical requirements, identified and resolved the gap areas before kick-starting Cloud migration. Owing to the scale and complexities involved, Forsys recommended a phased approach to migration, starting with Finance, to ensure all the teams are unaffected. The POC resulted in a hybrid Cloud-On Premise application landscape for the client and integrations were built between Finance on Cloud and the On-Premise modules, including third-party tools. Some of the key activities taken up by Forsys to ensure a smooth and seamless migration included: ▶ POC and Demo on Oracle Cloud (General Ledger (GL), accounts payable (AP), accounts receivable (AR), Fixed Assets (FA), Order Management (OM) and Inventory (INV) for two business units (the US and France), enabled France

The POC resulted in a hybrid Cloud-On Premise application landscape for the client and integrations were built between Finance on Cloud and the On-Premise modules, including third-party tools. Some of the key activities taken up by Forsys to ensure a smooth and seamless migration included: ▶ POC and Demo on Oracle Cloud (General Ledger (GL), accounts payable (AP), accounts receivable (AR), Fixed Assets (FA), Order Management (OM) and Inventory (INV) for two business units (the US and France), enabled France GAAP Rules. ▶ Setting up of the Financial Report Centre (FRC). ▶ Enabled Infolets in Oracle Fusion Applications to display key information about the client. ▶ Performed Data Conversion (GL Balances, Currency Conversion, Open AR Invoices, Open AP invoices, Customers, Suppliers and Assets). ▶ Successful integration of Master Data and Transactional Data. To prepare the client for cloud adoption, Forsys developed both short-term and long-term roadmaps and included them in the POC that empowered the client to achieve the below-mentioned results: ▶ Data readiness helped improve the implementation time by 25%. ▶ Quickly closed the existing and ongoing customizations. ▶ Forsys built Accelerators (Flodata) for Data Migration and Integrations with third-party tools helped save 30% cost and time. ▶ Drastically reduced month/quarter end process to one day. ▶ Optimized reporting center with contextual collaboration enabling anytime, anywhere access. ▶ Automated journal entries in the primary ledger to transact to the secondary ledger to ensure French GAAP requirements are met. ▶ Asset transactions could get reflected in the secondary ledger as soon as the transactions are interfaced to GL, as per the France Statutory requirements. ▶ Data cleansing, removing duplicates, filtering redundant and outdated data. ▶ Data reconciliation by performing validation within the source system as well as between source and destination. ▶ Moving reports from the Financial Statement Generator to Fusion Reporting Studio involved careful mapping between the On-Premise data and equivalent Cloud fields. ▶ Data (open and closed transactional data) analysis based on jurisdiction and regulations. forsysinc.com Follow us

16 Overview PURPOSE OF THIS REQUEST FOR PROPOSAL (RFP) We are seeking a qualified vendor to provide comprehensive support and ongoing enhancement services for our Oracle ERP environment. The selected vendor will be responsible for the ongoing support, maintenance, and optimization of our Oracle applications, including OIC, Field Service Cloud, CPQ Cloud, Sales Cloud, Demantra, and EBS as well as provide enhancement services. Key services include incident management, problem resolution, change management, performance tuning, patch management, and regular system upgrades. The vendor must demonstrate expertise in Oracle technologies, possess a proven track record of successful support and enhancement delivery, and offer a flexible support model that can adapt to our evolving business needs. Additionally, the vendor should provide proactive monitoring, detailed reporting, and continuous improvement initiatives to ensure the stability, security, and efficiency of our Oracle systems. The chosen vendor will work as an extension of our existing IT team, acting as level 1 & 2 support For EBS Tele Services, Field Service, Order Management , Service Contracts, Finance (AR, AP, FA, Cash Management , GL) and provide level 1-3 support for Demantra, CPQ, OFSC, Sales Cloud, CX Analytics, EB-Tax, Discrete Manufacturing, Purchasing and SPP, with option of supporting level 1-3

reporting, and continuous improvement initiatives to ensure the stability, security, and efficiency of our Oracle systems. The chosen vendor will work as an extension of our existing IT team, acting as level 1 & 2 support For EBS Tele Services, Field Service, Order Management , Service Contracts, Finance (AR, AP, FA, Cash Management , GL) and provide level 1-3 support for Demantra, CPQ, OFSC, Sales Cloud, CX Analytics, EB-Tax, Discrete Manufacturing, Purchasing and SPP, with option of supporting level 1-3 for all modules in future. COMPANY BACKGROUND Onto Innovation Inc. is a prominent player in the semiconductor industry, formed in 2019 through the merger of Rudolph Technologies, Inc. and Nanometrics Incorporated. Headquartered in Wilmington, Massachusetts, the company specializes in providing advanced process control equipment and software for microelectronic manufacturing industries. Onto Innovation's product portfolio includes automated defect inspection and metrology systems, probe card test and analysis systems, and lithography step-and-repeat systems. Onto provide both hardware and software solutions to its customers as well as robust services. The company is publicly traded on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol ONTO. Onto Innovation's commitment to innovation and customer collaboration is evident in its global presence and extensive portfolio. The company supports its customers with a worldwide sales and service organization, providing cutting-edge technologies such as unpatterned wafer quality, 3D metrology, macro defect inspection, metal interconnect composition, factory analytics, and advanced semiconductor packaging lithography. By optimizing customers' critical paths of progress, Onto Innovation helps them become smarter, faster, and more efficient, ultimately improving product quality and reliability. Onto Innovation employs approximately 1,520 people worldwide. The company has a significant global footprint, with operations and customer support centers in key regions including the United States, Southeast Asia, China, Japan, Europe, Taiwan, and South Korea. This extensive geographic presence enables Onto Innovation to effectively serve its diverse customer base and respond swiftly to the dynamic needs of the semiconductor industry. The company's Onto Innovation – ERP AMS RFP Onto Innovation Proprietary and Confidential Page 4 strategic locations also facilitate close collaboration with other semiconductor manufacturers, ensuring that Onto Innovation remains at the forefront of technological advancements and industry trends. Background High Level Background Onto Innovation’s business currently runs on the SAP, Oracle and SysPro ERP platforms. These platforms were in place prior to the merger of Rudolph and Nanometrics. Starting in 2024, Onto Innovation embarked on an ERP transformation codenamed Project Elevate. The objective of Project Elevate is to consolidate, streamline and automate Sales, Finance, Manufacturing and Service operations under one ERP. Project Elevate is planned to go live in June of 2025. In parallel to the Oracle ERP transformation, Onto is enabling Master Data Management and a mix of near real time and daily batch integrations through the Flow Data Platform as well as a revised Data and BI Ecosystem which is comprised of Oracle ECC, Microsoft SQL Server (On Premise) and Power BI. We are also enabling a new 3PL strategy. Ecosystem Overview The post Elevate ERP Ecosystem will be a hybrid SaaS, on premise solutions with Oracle EBS 12.2.11 at the core along with Oracle Integration Cloud, Oracle Field Services Cloud, Oracle CPQ and Oracle Sales Cloud , Oracle Demantra, Oracle EPM as well as Pico MES and PTC Windchill PLM. For the purposes of this RFP, Pico and Windchill are out of scope. forsysinc.com Business Challenge As a scaling organization, the absence of a standardized and accurate Order-to-Cash process impacted SFDC adoption, delayed quoting, caused issues in contract visibility and operational efficiency, hindered order & invoice processing, and hampered the ease of doing business. Moreover, the process of advancing sales from lead to close and converting revenue to cash was slow, complex, and relied on manual intervention, which risked sales, delayed revenue, and cash flow. Over 30k Reduction in Product Proliferation with Order-to-Cash Optimization Resulting in Touchless Order Process Insight A leading provider of enterprise open source solutions wanted to streamline and optimize its existing Order-to-Cash process due to challenges like the inability to create competitive quotes, inaccurate invoices, delayed payments, etc. Forsys engaged with the client to offer assessment & advisory expertise, which comprised discovery, analysis, and assessing current processes and systems to document the ‘as-is’ state; designing and reviewing the ‘to-be’ processes; and creating the CPQ & CLM implementation roadmap. As a result, the client drastically reduced product proliferation from 36K to 3K+ and price list proliferation from 200+ to 15+. Mapped out resource use. Designed high-level change management & cut over strategy for Conga CPQ & CLM Identified and suggested risk management strategy Outlined the change request process. Reduction in product proliferation from 36K to 3000+ Lowering of price list proliferation from 200+ to 15+ Automation of orders & reduction of Quote-to-Order errors Bringing down the order touch rate and order cycle time Minimization of customer inquiries. Forsys has been recognized by Great Place to Work® India among the Top 50 - India’s Best Workplaces™ for Women 2021 (Mid-size Companies Category)! As a global authority on workplace culture, Great Place to Work® has been studying employee experience and people practices across organizations for over three decades. Every year, more than 10,000 organizations from over 60 countries partner with Great Place to Work® Institute for assessment, benchmarking and planning of actions to strengthen their workplace culture. In India, the research conducted by Great Place to Work® has focused on the state of trust in workplaces, what drives employee experience, and its impact on the business outcomes. The research reveals that there is a clear and direct relationship between employee engagement and financial performance, and that best workplaces consistently outperform major stock indices in India. “One thing the Best Workplaces™ for Women have taught us is that irrespective of the challenges posed by the pandemic or even the industry they operate in – they are intentional about building a diverse, equitable and inclusive environment. Voices of their women employees, the quality of inclusive practices and actions taken towards equitable distribution of power within the organization is a testimony to that,” said Sandhya Ramesh, Head of the Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Practice at Great Place to Work® India. Forsys & Salesforce have inked collaboration to address digital transformation challenges affecting businesses. Through this strategic alliance, the technology leaders set the stage for technological innovations that will enable businesses to create world-class customer experiences while maximizing ROI. CALIFORNIA – July 25, 2022 – Forsys, a leader in Lead-to-Revenue (LTR) business and technology consulting, expanded its partnership strength with Salesforce by becoming a Consulting and ISV partner for the entire Salesforce Customer 360 platform, including the Revenue Cloud. This partnership will make it easy for organizations to leverage the joint competencies of Salesforce and Forsys to build, design, and deploy solutions and undertake business transformation confidently. Forsys comes with a proven track record of delivering numerous trailblazer success stories & executing 200+ Salesforce engagements in CPQ, Billing, Order Management, CRM, CLM, Revenue & Digital Commerce for global enterprises in the past 5 years alone, using a global team of 120+ Certified Navigators in Sales Cloud & CPQ.
